item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is stuck between a main list and -J scip. It's not really interesting enough to be taken seriously but it's not funny enough to be taken as a joke. The fact that it self neutralised almost seems appropriate given its lack of function or purpose. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I say it should be one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: with a few tweaks, this could easily be rewritten into a -j article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is interesting, but there is no such thing as “anointing with the blessings of Confucius”

I think that's the point of the phrase, to shed skepticism/suspicion about the claims made that the object is a "genuine Buddhist artifact"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is interesting, but there is no such thing as “anointing with the blessings of Confucius” in Chinese culture. Anointing is something that exists in Abraham religions. (Correct me if I said anything wrong)

Basically, Confucius is worshipped as a sage and an expert on education, and he has nothing to do with religious blessings. Blessings exists in folk believe, Taoism and Buddism, but mostly they bless some objects that can be used as a talisman.

Since this is something that does not change what is going on in the scp, I guess the author may want to fix this…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally the whole thing is just confusing . So it's a fortune cookie that leaks liquid and makes paper appear . I don't get it . The messages from inside the cookie seem ridiculous as well .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
collected liquid is to be disposed as Level 1 Anomalous waste

the liquid released by SCP-1335 has not been found to possess any anomalous properties.

If it's not anomalous, why do they treat it as anomalous waste?

I really liked this because of how absurd the whole shebang is. And I would like to point out that even if "infinite liquid" scips abound, in this specific case it makes sense. It wasn't just thrown over a finished scip to get onto the bandwagon.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Down vote. I can't begin to imagine the story here and its not really funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you use the word "standard" to describe everything in your article from toothpicks to disinformation campaigns to fortune cookies you lack understanding of how that word should be used. I downvote due to stylistic problems, overuse and misuse of "standard" and an overall lackluster article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for future edits. Making a mental note to reread and evaluate when I come across it in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cookie is a duplex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huehuehue, that was my plan all along! Bwahahaha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the premise, though it does feel a lot like the rewritten 619. A tentative upvote, though I do think slightly more clarification as to the actual setup might be helpful - seems like there's two entities in 1335, whatever's causing the phenoma and the 'neighbour' who produces the message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn you.
DARN YOU.

Now I can ONLY think Dave or Bro wrote them XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I liked the whole restaurant segment. Read to me like a standard "Mystery Spot" dumb tourist attractor, exactly what I would expect of any small business owner who happened upon such an oddity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having read this for the first time after all your refinements, I'd say your effort was worth it. Cheerfully upvoted. A savory, bite-sized SCP; the fortunes read like haiku to me.

The date redactions should be reworked, though; either reveal the month or also redact the day. Leaving the day only conveys nothing, and looks weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the notes, but I don't like the "genuine Buddhist artifact" part of the backstory.

I actually think this would fit into the joke or anomalous categories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta agree with Roget. I just read it after revisions, and while it can be the hardest thing at this point to rewrite, I'm not quite into it.

Also, I think infinite liquid is present on the site enough already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's not actually true; fortune cookies are originally Japanese, all the way back to the 19th Century. There are a few differences:

  • The fortunes were originally in the bend, not folded into the cookie.
  • The cookies were soy miso based, not vanilla and butter.
  • They are called 辻占煎餅: fortune-telling rice crackers.
  • They're larger and darker in color.

You can still buy them near the Fushimi Inari-taisha shrine in Kyoto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All right, that's been clarified. But what happened to the rest of it? The first paragraph of the description is a little off (the second sentence in particular) and you put the recovery information second, before the full description of what the anomaly is. I'm fairly sure it wasn't like that yesterday, anyway. The logical flow should be to have the anomaly fully described before getting into anything else.

Also, something I wondered about yesterday: does the water have any effect on the fortune cookie? It might be worth noting this, since I assume it doesn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the addendum, was I the only one to envision Dave strider writing the notes? I dunno, probably is just me, what with the fandom clusterfuck in my head 24/7.

Anyways, upvote.

Although there is room for improvement, as stated in earlier comments, I'd say keep the addendum as is. It's a good stress relief scip, with all these grimdark ones floating around that kill, mutilate, and otherwise fuck shit up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of upvoting for potential, I'm downvoting so you're more motivated to fix things. I badly want to upvote this, so if you can make the article tie together more coherently I would happily switch my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not entirely sure what I just read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The big guys pretty much stated most of the things that I'm apprehensive about. Your clarification of the Confucius misinformation, while it does clear some things up, reminds me of a person telling a joke that no one really laughs at so you explain it, everyone goes "oh I get it now" but they still won't laugh because the joke didn't have the proper structure to give real punch. Neutral vote with the hopes of future improvements

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After careful consideration…

I simply don't care for it. The notes you've written are silly enough, and the mechanics aren't terrible, but rolled up together, the package just isn't something I can stand behind. It starts off going in the right direction, and then squanders all of the potential for weirdness on something as simple as a tenant rivalry.

While I don't explicitly hate it, I don't think this piece adds sufficient value to the site that I'd be upset or feel something was lost if it was gone. So I'm sorry to say that I have given this piece a -1.

You've got potential though. I'm curious to see what you come up with next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like weird little skips that are basically useless. The Foundation does not discriminate when acquiring SCPs, and would definitely investigate any religious artifacts. I can also see the restaurant advertising this to increase business. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I already liked this as a draft.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, too, am also going to upvote for potential, because I want to see this cleaned up and present on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvote with a bit of apprehension. Like TroyL, I'm voting for potential, but at the moment, it needs to be refined.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm gonna toss you a +1 for a while, but I expect to see this one cleaned up and fixed. My vote is for potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but I can't up vote it in good faith until the errors everyone has already mentioned are addressed. Also, how did they remove the strips of paper? You made it seem like a big deal before the addendum, but don't address it at all. Just saying "they did it yay" gives no closure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the note within SCP-XXXX was removed. Upon examination, it was discovered to contain the following message:

The next day, the following message was discovered and retrieved from within SCP-XXXX.

…were you too busy posting this to see my feedback? I was wondering how there was more then one message from this SCP if you said there was only one.
Edit: I see that you said the second one was retrieved, but you should mention that the SCP will manifest strips of paper inside of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't necessarily approve of it, but so long as it's not just general dumbassery I guess I'll begrudgingly give it a pass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bad real SCPs do not make good joke SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The incorrect fact is intentional

EDIT: To clarify, that line was sort of my personal joke on the crappy state of most "chinese" food places.

I am Chinese. I've had real Chinese food. And in my experience, most places that give you a fortune cookie, don't serve you that. So I wouldn't expect the owner of one of those places to know the difference between Confucianism and Buddhism. I wouldn't even expect him to know who Confucius was, except that the name sounds exotic.

So, if its a bad joke, if its not funny, or too obtuse, or doesn't fit with the rest of the article, then tell me, and I'll fix it. However, know that I do know what I'm talking about

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foundation agents discovered SCP-1335 in a fountain being displayed as a "genuine Buddhist artifact", with employees encouraging customers to "anoint themselves with the blessings of Confucius".

No, no, a thousand times no. Confucius was a Chinese philosopher and teacher, and the Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama) was a Nepalese religious teacher who taught primarily in Northern India, and they each had different schools of thought (Confucianism and Buddhism, respectively). Confucius taught personal/governmental morality and correctness in social relationships, and Gautama taught enlightenment through elimination of ignorance and desire. The point of this is, Buddhists would never have anything to do with a religious display with a tagline having to do with the "blessings of Confucius," because Confucius has nary a thing to do with Buddhism. Please please please check your facts a little more before writing your next article.

That aside, I think the article itself could use a bit more fleshing out. Expand on the note aspect, give a few more instances of that, and clean up the tone, and I could end up liking this. Until then, downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This might do a bit better as a joke SCP, but it might not do too well as that either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the way the Foundation found out about the skip. Was that the only part that bothered you, or is it the entire backstory?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a little of SCP-619, but this is amusing without being unnecessarily long or giving away everything. So I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which skip do you think it's too similar to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels like something we've had before, but the other one was better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's still pretty bad. I don't think the Foundation would go to somewhere claiming to have a real religious artifact. Pretty sure they'd be raiding at least five places a day in every city in the world if they did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eeeh, it's kind on an edge of a cliff for me.

Neutral.. for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They were still trying to find a way to remove the notes when the description was written. They found one by the first addendum.

Clarified in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was skeptical at first, then I read the notes.

+1, although you should consider expanding the Containment Procedures and Description, as their length seems to be dragging an otherwise funny skip down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's not quite enough here, I think. It just feels a bit sparse. The main question I have is, if they can't get the papers out without destroying the cookie, how the heck do they have those notes?

That said, the notes are great, the perfect kind of tone to elevate this to something a bit more than what it is. But don't pluralize unit abbreviations (mL, not mLs).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It turned out to be better then I thought when I started reading. But I feel it still lacks something, so +0 for now. What does the liquid do? It does not appear to have special properties, so why do people annoint themselves at the restaurant? This couls be better developped or explained.

Some specifics:
Redaction in procedures. There's no reason why people can't know where this is stored.

100 mLs .

no need for s on units (at first I wondered if it was ml/s

The liquid expelled from SCP-1335 is composed primarily of liquid water

''liquid'' water is redundant here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny (in the good way) and while there are minor issues, it would be great if this stays here… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never read this one before, but I'm throwing my two cents in. Wasn't really sure how to feel about this, wonder if it would work better as a joke? It's weird, but it's a very, safe sounding, very friendly story. Not that everything needs to rip someone's arm off or turn them into goo, but it just reads, very nonsensical? Dunno. :I Neutral nonvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are several plae where it still says SCP-XXX or xx/06/xx. That aside I don't find it particularly appealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rewrote the backstory a little.

Better? Worse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just silly. I could start a rumor of a magical fortune cookie by bullshitting with my friends. It's not exactly credible intelligence. You need to think of what's believable in the context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea, but the execution leaves something to be desired. Neutral vote for the time being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this as a story, but I'm not sure if it fits as an SCP. It almost seems more like an anomalous item to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a mental image of a sort of fountain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What specifically is wrong with the backstory? Is the section fixable, or should I just remove it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fortune cookies are an American invention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description is really rather poor here. Though the notes are amusing and the idea behind this might work, I can't upvote it as it stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've upvoted because I found the notes amusing, but this is littered with loads of small errors that could be easily fixed. The backstory (rumors of a "magical fortune cookie") stuck out in particular as being pretty bad.

E: Actually, sorry. I'm gonna downvote, but only because I want to see a better version of this with the notes.
